role_adherence_notes.linguistic: Focused on English text and logistics terminology.
role_adherence_notes.botanics: Identified desert scrub and xeric plants.
role_adherence_notes.regulatory: Evaluated road markings and traffic flow.
role_adherence_notes.meta: Synthesized truck design and infrastructure cues.

visual_consistency_notes.linguistic: Text on truck matches image perfectly.
visual_consistency_notes.landscape: Desert terrain and mountains match image.
visual_consistency_notes.regulatory: Road markings and driving side match image.

verdict.overall_verdict_notes: The agents provided a highly consistent and accurate analysis. The linguistic and regulatory evidence (English text, .com URL, right-hand traffic, US-style truck) strongly points to the US. The landscape and botanics agents correctly identified the Sonoran Desert environment, which spans the US-Mexico border, but the linguistic/regulatory evidence effectively ruled out Mexico. The judge correctly synthesized these signals to identify the US as the only plausible candidate.
verdict.region_narrowing_notes: The judge correctly identified a strong consensus for North America (specifically the US) based on the linguistic and regulatory evidence, while appropriately noting the landscape/botanical overlap with Mexico. The decision to narrow to the US was sound.
verdict.hypothesis_pool_notes: The pool only included the United States. While the US is the correct answer, a more robust pool would have included Mexico as a plausible alternative given the landscape/botanical evidence, allowing the linguistic/regulatory evidence to definitively rule it out.
